metadata.summary: Cloud Run observability: structured JSON logging, Cloud Monitoring dashboards, SLO alerting, Cloud Trace propagation, gcloud log queries for services and jobs, and cost optimization monitoring.

content.title: Cloud Run Monitoring and Observability
content.summary: Cloud Run exports metrics to Cloud Monitoring automatically (request count, latency, instance count, CPU/memory utilization). Applications MUST emit structured JSON logs to stdout/stderr to enable Cloud Logging parsing. Cloud Trace integrates via OpenTelemetry or the Cloud Trace client library. Configure alerting policies on error rate and p99 latency for production SLOs.

why.p: Cloud Run's built-in metrics cover infrastructure signals (instance count, CPU, memory) but not application-level signals (error rates per endpoint, business transaction latency, queue depth). Structured JSON logging enables field-based filtering and log-based metrics in Cloud Monitoring. Without structured logs, error investigation requires full-text search across unstructured output, which is slow and unreliable at scale.

list.item: Setting up structured logging for a Cloud Run service or job.
Creating Cloud Monitoring dashboards and alerting policies for Cloud Run.
Configuring Cloud Trace for distributed tracing across Cloud Run services.
Debugging service errors, latency spikes, or scaling issues via logs and metrics.
Defining SLOs and error budgets for production Cloud Run services.
Monitoring Cloud Run job executions and failed tasks.
VPC and network configuration — see cloud-run-vpc-access.
Autoscaling tuning based on metrics — see cloud-run-autoscaling.
GKE workload monitoring — use Workload Identity + Prometheus instead.

rule.statement: Cloud Run applications MUST write structured JSON logs to stdout (not stderr for normal operation). JSON logs MUST include at minimum: severity (matching Cloud Logging severity levels: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL), message, and a request trace ID for request correlation. Do NOT include sensitive data (credentials, PII) in logs. Set LOG_LEVEL via environment variable, not hardcoded.
rule.rationale: Cloud Logging auto-parses JSON from stdout and extracts fields into the structured log entry. Unstructured text logs require manual parsing, cannot be filtered by field, and cannot generate log-based metrics. Trace ID correlation lets Cloud Logging link log entries to Cloud Trace spans for distributed request tracing.

example.title: Python structured JSON logging

antipattern.description: Using print() statements or unstructured logger.info("user_id: " + user_id) for application logging.
antipattern.reason: Unstructured logs cannot be filtered by field (e.g., user_id = "u123"), cannot generate log-based metrics, and are 10x slower to search in Cloud Logging. Structured JSON is parsed at ingest with zero extra cost.

example.title: gcloud log queries for Cloud Run service
example.code:
# Service logs (last 100 lines, formatted) gcloud logging read \ "resource.type=cloud_run_revision AND resource.labels.service_name=my-service" \ --limit=100 \ --format="table(timestamp, textPayload)"
# Error logs only gcloud logging read \ "resource.type=cloud_run_revision AND resource.labels.service_name=my-service AND severity>=ERROR" \ --limit=50
# Stream logs in real-time gcloud beta run services logs tail my-service --region=us-central1
# Job task failure logs gcloud logging read \ "resource.type=cloud_run_job AND resource.labels.job_name=my-job" \ --limit=100
# Log-based metric: error count in last 24h gcloud logging read \ "resource.type=cloud_run_revision AND resource.labels.service_name=my-service AND severity=ERROR" \ --freshness=24h \ --format="value(timestamp)" | wc -l

rule.statement: Configure alerting policies on these key metrics for every production Cloud Run service: run.googleapis.com/request/count filtered by response_code_class=5xx (error rate), run.googleapis.com/request/latencies at 99th percentile, run.googleapis.com/container/instance_count (to detect scaling events), and run.googleapis.com/container/cpu/utilizations. Set notification channels to PagerDuty, email, or Pub/Sub before launch.
rule.rationale: Cloud Run emits these metrics without instrumentation. A 5xx rate spike is the primary signal for deployment failures. p99 latency captures tail latency which affects the worst 1% of users — often caused by cold starts, memory pressure, or downstream timeouts. Instance count spikes indicate unexpected traffic bursts that may need autoscaling limit adjustments.

example.title: Create alerting policy for 5xx error rate (gcloud)
example.code: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create \ --display-name="Cloud Run my-service 5xx rate > 1%" \ --condition-display-name="5xx rate" \ --condition-filter='resource.type="cloud_run_revision" AND resource.labels.service_name="my-service" AND metric.type="run.googleapis.com/request/count" AND metric.labels.response_code_class="5xx"' \ --condition-threshold-value=0.01 \ --condition-threshold-duration=60s \ --condition-comparison=COMPARISON_GT \ --notification-channels="projects/my-project/notificationChannels/my-channel"

example.title: Key Cloud Monitoring metrics for Cloud Run
example.code:
# Request metrics run.googleapis.com/request/count -- by response_code_class (2xx/3xx/4xx/5xx) run.googleapis.com/request/latencies -- percentiles: p50, p95, p99 run.googleapis.com/pending_queue/pending_requests -- queued requests waiting for instances
# Instance metrics run.googleapis.com/container/instance_count -- active instances per revision run.googleapis.com/container/startup_latencies -- cold start duration
# Resource metrics run.googleapis.com/container/cpu/utilizations -- CPU usage per instance run.googleapis.com/container/memory/utilizations -- memory usage per instance
# Job metrics run.googleapis.com/job/completed_task_attempt_count -- by result (succeeded/failed) run.googleapis.com/job/running_task_attempt_count

rule.statement: Enable distributed tracing by propagating the X-Cloud-Trace-Context header through all service-to-service calls. Use the Cloud Trace client library or OpenTelemetry SDK to create custom spans for database queries, external API calls, and critical business logic. Set trace sampling to 1% for high-volume services, 100% for low-volume or during incident investigation.
rule.rationale: Cloud Run automatically instruments HTTP request spans in Cloud Trace when the X-Cloud-Trace-Context header is present. Without header propagation, cross-service traces break into disconnected spans, making root cause analysis of latency issues across microservices impossible.

example.title: Trace header propagation in Python
Cloud Billing queries for Cloud Run cost analysis
example.code:
# Get Cloud Run spend by service (BigQuery billing export required) bq query --use_legacy_sql=false ' SELECT resource.labels.service_name AS service, SUM(cost) AS total_cost, SUM(usage.amount) AS total_usage, usage.unit FROM `my-project.billing.gcp_billing_export_resource_v1_XXXXX` WHERE service.description = "Cloud Run" AND DATE(usage_start_time) >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL 30 DAY) GROUP BY 1, 4 ORDER BY 2 DESC '
# Identify services with high min-instance cost (idle instances) gcloud monitoring time-series list \ --filter='metric.type="run.googleapis.com/container/instance_count" AND resource.labels.service_name!=""' \ --format="table(resource.labels.service_name, points[0].value.int64Value)"
example.note: Services with instance_count greater than 0 during off-hours are incurring min-instance charges. Evaluate setting min-instances=0 for non-critical services.

checklist.check: Structured JSON logging implemented (all log entries are JSON to stdout).
Log levels appropriate (DEBUG for dev, INFO for prod; no DEBUG in prod default).
Request IDs and trace IDs propagated and included in log entries.
Sensitive data excluded from logs (no passwords, tokens, PII).
Cloud Monitoring dashboards created with key metrics.
Alerting policies configured for 5xx error rate and p99 latency.
SLOs defined and monitored in Cloud Monitoring.
Uptime checks configured for public service endpoints.
Cloud Trace enabled and X-Cloud-Trace-Context propagated.
Log-based metrics created for application-level error patterns.
Notification channels configured before service goes live.
